name: Dark Console
summary: Terminal-native dark system with warm/cool black surfaces, mono labels, code panels, status accents, and command rhythm.

style_dna.philosophy: A working console, not a generic dark theme: every panel should feel executable.
style_dna.visual_signature: Dark canvas, command blocks, mono captions, status green, and subtle bordered layers.
style_dna.surface_logic: Stack void, panel, elevated command, and active status states.

palette.accent_policy: Green marks execution, current state, success, or selected path only.
palette.semantic_policy: Add amber/red only for warnings/errors in operational content.

layout.grid: Panel-based command layout with code blocks, split panes, and status rows.
layout.whitespace: Compact groups with clear command/output separation.
layout.image_treatment: Product UI, terminal screenshots, and code frames.

components.buttons: Dark pills or command-line prompts with active accent.
components.cards: Code panels with hairline borders and subtle inner contrast.
components.tables: Log-like rows with mono IDs and status chips.

slides.accent_rule: Use green for active command, status, and terminal highlights.

document.heading_rule: Printable docs stay light, but code blocks carry dark-console rhythm.

source_inheritance.method: Synthesized from local getdesign.md working corpus.

do: Use mono labels for commands, states, and IDs.
Keep contrast accessible on dark surfaces.
dont: Do not use random neon colors.
Do not make long Word docs dark by default.
Do not fake code screenshots for final text.

pptx: Use deterministic HTML/CSS or PPTX text boxes for final text.
Keep one core idea per slide and validate rendered PNG dimensions.
docx: Translate visual style into Word typography, margins, heading hierarchy, tables, and callouts.
Keep documents printable unless the user explicitly requests PDF-only visual art direction.
For DOCX, keep page white but render code blocks with console-inspired styling.
carousel: Use high-resolution fixed canvas, mobile-readable text, and safe margins.
Make each frame complete enough to stand alone.
